title: rubus longebracteatus
intro: Rubus longebracteatus, also known as the Taiwanese raspberry or wild raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, native to Taiwan. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthocyanins and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Rubus longebracteatus include its potential to prevent oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Rubus longebracteatus is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
